verses.1: A Song of Ascents. Remember, LORD, in David’s behalf, All his affliction;
verses.2: How he swore to the LORD And vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob,
verses.3: “I certainly will not enter my house, Nor lie on my bed;
verses.4: I will not give sleep to my eyes Or slumber to my eyelids,
verses.5: Until I find a place for the LORD, A dwelling place for the Mighty One of Jacob.”
verses.6: Behold, we heard about it in Ephrathah, We found it in the field of Jaar.
verses.7: Let’s go into His dwelling place; Let’s worship at His footstool.
verses.8: Arise, LORD, to Your resting place, You and the ark of Your strength.
verses.9: May Your priests be clothed with righteousness, And may Your godly ones sing for joy.
verses.10: For the sake of Your servant David, Do not turn away the face of Your anointed.
verses.11: The LORD has sworn to David A truth from which He will not turn back: “I will set upon your throne one from the fruit of your body.
verses.12: “If your sons will keep My covenant And My testimony which I will teach them, Their sons also will sit upon your throne forever.”
verses.13: For the LORD has chosen Zion; He has desired it as His dwelling place.
verses.14: “This is My resting place forever; Here I will dwell, for I have desired it.
verses.15: “I will abundantly bless her food; I will satisfy her needy with bread.
verses.16: “I will also clothe her priests with salvation, And her godly ones will sing aloud for joy.
verses.17: “I will make the horn of David spring forth there; I have prepared a lamp for My anointed.
verses.18: “I will clothe his enemies with shame, But upon himself his crown will gleam.”
